basics.name: Vadim Nicolai
basics.label: Senior Full Stack Engineer
basics.summary: Senior Full Stack Engineer with 12+ years of experience building scalable web applications using React, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Expert in modern frontend architectures, microservices, GraphQL APIs, and performance-critical systems. Delivering 5 AI-powered projects with multi-model LLM orchestration, agentic pipelines, RAG search, eval-driven development, and production observability. Domain expertise in E-commerce, FinTech, AI/ML, and enterprise SaaS.
basics.keySkills: React • TypeScript • JavaScript • Next.js • Node.js • Go • Rust • GraphQL • Apollo • REST API • Microservices • gRPC • AWS • Cloudflare • MongoDB • DynamoDB • Docker • CI/CD • Jest • Git • AI/LLM Orchestration

work.name: Vitrifi
work.summary:
• Architected micro-frontend portal using Go microservices with gRPC and OpenAPI, integrating shared React components across multiple teams.
  • Led API migration from REST to GraphQL, implementing Apollo Server in Go and Apollo Client in React, reducing over-fetching and improving performance.
  • Drove frontend architecture and technical leadership across teams, establishing design patterns and enforcing quality through PR reviews.
  • Migrated monorepo from npm to PNPM with Turborepo caching, reducing CI/CD build times.
  • Integration testing strategy with Jest and React Testing Library, automated in CI/CD.
  • Tech stack: Go, Echo, gRPC, GraphQL, Apollo Server, OpenAPI, React, TypeScript, Apollo Client, React Query, Ant Design, Turborepo, PNPM, MongoDB, Jest, React Testing Library

work.name: Wunderman Thompson Commerce

  • SSR with Next.js for SEO and page load performance.

  • Tech stack: React 17, Next.js 10, TypeScript, Styled Components, Jest, React Testing Library

work.name: Event Espresso

aiProjects.name: Agentic Lead Gen
aiProjects.description: Autonomous B2B lead generation platform with a 5-stage agentic pipeline (Discovery → Enrichment → Contact Hunter + QA Auditor → Outreach Composer), type-safe GraphQL API on Neon Postgres, 51 LangGraph graphs running on Cloudflare Workers Containers with AsyncPostgresSaver checkpointing, Candle/Metal BGE-M3 embeddings on the local hot path (zero outbound calls), and a 7-rule strategy enforcer that gates every prompt/model change.
highlights: 5-stage pipeline: Discovery → Enrichment → Contact Hunter + QA Auditor (parallel) → Outreach Composer with plan-approval gate.
Apollo Server 5 GraphQL: 23 resolvers, 18 DataLoaders (2 ms batch), Drizzle ORM + Neon Postgres, per-mutation admin guards.
51 LangGraph graphs (5 core + 46 specialized): same Python under `langgraph dev` (in-memory) and Cloudflare Workers Containers (FastAPI + AsyncPostgresSaver on Neon).
DeepSeek cheap-first routing: v4-flash for summary nodes, v4-pro reasoner (thinking + reasoning_effort=high) for value-metric / pricing / GTM nodes; per-node cost telemetry.
Candle/Metal BGE-M3 (1024-dim) embeddings via a Rust crate, stored as pgvector with HNSW; product × company top-K via a single SQL JOIN.
Contact-tier LoRA: Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct + custom adapter, Q4_K_M GGUF on llama-cpp on a free HuggingFace Space, gated by an authority_score ≥ 0.5 prefilter (~10× compute cut).
Aho-Corasick company classifier (O(n+m+z) single linear pass) replaces O(n × k) substring scans and removes an LLM call from the hot path; DeepSeek v4-flash JSON-mode fallback.

Hand-tuned ML: 12-feature gradient-boosted stumps (sub-ms); Hawkes lead temperature with O(n) recursive form + 1024-entry exp-decay LUT; Thompson-sampling send-time over 168 Beta posteriors; Kaplan-Meier follow-up cadence.
Bidirectional email: contact_emails (Resend, In-Reply-To + References) + received_emails (HMAC-SHA256-verified Svix webhook, no SDK); CPN forwarding alias on one MX catch-all — zero per-contact DNS.

6-channel GitHub recruitment crawler in Rust (15-pass bio search, stargazers, contributors, org members, follower graph, seed domains); 50+ fields per candidate in one GraphQL query; 9-component rising-score.

7-rule strategy enforcer (eval ≥80%, grounding BLOCKING on un-schemaed LLM calls, taxonomy, multi-model, spec, observability, HITL); session-end hook scores Claude Code transcripts and queues low scorers for self-improvement.

aiProjects.name: ResearchThera.com
aiProjects.description: Therapeutic + clinical-intelligence platform for families. LlamaIndex-powered healthcare RAG ingests lab PDFs into 8 pgvector tables with 7 derived clinical ratios and a 5-stage local-only clinical chat pipeline; a 7-node LangGraph research generator surfaces evidence-based therapeutic content across 7 academic sources. 6 AI agents (3 Python LangGraph + 1 Python LlamaIndex + 2 AI SDK) over a GraphQL API.
highlights: Healthcare RAG: PDFs → LlamaParse → 3-tier BloodTestNodeParser (HTML / Romanian FormKeysValues / free-text) → FastEmbed bge-large-en-v1.5 (1024-dim ONNX) → blood_test + N blood_marker + health_state nodes.

7 clinical ratios/upload, JSONB on health_state, authored thresholds: TG/HDL (McLaughlin), TC/HDL (Castelli), HDL/LDL (Millán), NLR (Fest), De Ritis AST/ALT, BUN/Creatinine (Hosten), TyG (Simental-Mendía).

5-stage LlamaIndex chat (no LangGraph): triage → re_triage (<0.7, ≤2) → route → ContextChatEngine (Similarity + ClinicalRelevance + CompactAndRefine) → guard. 9 intents, CompositeRetriever fan-out, k-scaling (≥0.8 / ≥0.6 1.5× / else 2×).
8 pgvector tables (blood_test, blood_marker FTS+vector, health_state, condition, medication, symptom, appointment, allergy) each with a BaseRetriever; search_marker_trend for longitudinal.
Medication deep research LangGraph → 6 pharmacology tables (pharmacology, indications, dosing, adverse_events, interactions, correlations) → /medications/[slug].
Deep issue analysis LangGraph: thematic clusters, timeline + family-system insights, root-cause, research-mapped priorities.
AI SDK (generateObject + Zod): storyTeller (3-part CYOA) + therapeutic (CBT/ACT/DBT/MBSR/LEGO) → R2.
7-node therapy research LangGraph (loadContext → normalizeGoal → planQuery → search → enrichAbstracts → extractAll → persist): 7 sources → DeepSeek + 0.72 gate → top 20 as 1024-dim pgvector.
~70-table Drizzle/Postgres schema: therapeutic core (goals, issues, habits, journals, claims) + 32 healthcare tables including 8 pgvector embedding tables.

volunteer.summary:
• Contributed 100+ pull requests to core trading engine and exchange connectivity modules in Rust and Python.
  • Built production exchange adapters for dYdX v4 and Hyperliquid with REST, WebSocket, gRPC, authentication, rate limiting, and execution clients.
  • Enhanced OKX and Kraken integrations with historical data support, pagination, and WebSocket reliability improvements.
  • Implemented high-performance technical indicators in Rust with Python/Cython parity, improving PnL calculations and monetary precision.
